evals.expected_output: Extracts knowledge from Airtable bases into a per-run cache as linked entity pages with per-batch Opus reviewer gate and mandatory HITL re-anchor on every new base; user reviews and merges into the vault.
expectations: Names Airtable bases as the source
Names linked entity pages as the destination shape
Mentions the per-batch Opus 4.6 reviewer gate at ≥95%
Mentions mandatory HITL re-anchor on every new base
Mentions that pages stage in ~/.claude/airtable-extract-cache, not directly in the vault

evals.prompt: How is /airtable-knowledge-extract launched? Can Claude run it from the current session?
evals.expected_output: No — Pattern B pipeline. Claude prints the supaterm tab invocation (env -u CLAUDECODE bash -c '...' inside `sp tab new --script`) and stops in-session. The bun runtime in run.mts owns the per-base loop; running a 30–60 min orchestration in-session burns ambient Opus context-tokens.
expectations: States the skill is Pattern B (refuse to run in-session)
Mentions launching via supaterm tab (sp tab new --script)
Mentions stripping CLAUDECODE from env (env -u CLAUDECODE) so child workers run outside the parent session
Mentions that Claude prints the invocation block and stops in-session, not Bash it

evals.prompt: Where do entity pages and the outcome log land?
evals.expected_output: Entity pages: ~/.claude/airtable-extract-cache/<run-id>/bases/<base-id>/out/<entity-type>/<slug>.md (frontmatter + [[wikilinks]]). Outcome log: <vault>/daily/skill-outcomes/airtable-knowledge-extract.log per skill-spec § 11, appended by run.mts CLI main().
expectations: Entity pages path includes ~/.claude/airtable-extract-cache/<run-id>/bases/<base-id>/out/<entity-type>
Mentions frontmatter + wikilinks shape on entity pages
Outcome log path is <vault>/daily/skill-outcomes/airtable-knowledge-extract.log
References skill-spec § 11 as the format spec
Notes that run.mts (not a separate skill ceremony) does the append

evals.prompt: What happens when the per-batch reviewer fails?
evals.expected_output: Self-improver appends a (raw, corrected, reason) triple to the per-base examples.jsonl, then retries the batch at half parallelism (max(1, floor(P/2))). Two consecutive fail-after-retry outcomes → SIGTERM workers + osascript notify + exit kind=escalated-batch.
expectations: Mentions the (raw, corrected, reason) triple appended to examples.jsonl
Retry runs at half parallelism
Two consecutive fail-after-retry outcomes escalate the rung
Escalation kills workers (SIGTERM) and notifies the user

evals.prompt: How does /airtable-knowledge-extract decide which Airtable bases to process?
evals.expected_output: scripts/active-bases.mts wraps list-bases.mts and applies the rules from references/base-selection-rules.md: inclusion via the operator allowlist at ~/.brain-os/airtable-premium-bases.txt (plain ID, ~regex on name, !override force-include, # comments), then 4 exclusions — archive / copy / test name regexes, and an inactive probe that drops bases with no record createdTime ≥ <current-year>-01-01. Trace JSON at ~/.claude/airtable-extract-cache/<run-id>/active-bases.json captures every raw base + verdict. Stdout (or --ids flag) feeds the per-base loop in SKILL.md ## Run.
expectations: Names scripts/active-bases.mts as the entry point that decides the set
Mentions the allowlist file at ~/.brain-os/airtable-premium-bases.txt
Mentions the !-prefix override that force-includes a base
Mentions the four exclusion rules (archive, copy, test, inactive)
Mentions the activity cutoff of <current-year>-01-01 (with AIRTABLE_ACTIVITY_CUTOFF override)
Mentions the trace JSON at ~/.claude/airtable-extract-cache/<run-id>/active-bases.json
Mentions that re-running the same run-id reads the cached trace and skips API probes

evals.prompt: How does the operator review the AI-drafted rubric questions before the diff is scored?
evals.expected_output: By default scripts/rubric-author.mts runs an interactive readline grill: one question at a time with a sample record from the airtable scan, accepting k (keep), d (drop), free-text rewrite, '+' to jump to add-mode (remaining auto-kept), or batch shorthand like 'k 1-5 d 6,7' (each range needs explicit k/d prefix). After the question loop, the operator is invited to add new questions until an empty line. Pass --editor to fall back to the original $EDITOR-based flow for non-interactive contexts. Prompts go to stderr so the SKILL.md supaterm `> /dev/null` redirect doesn't suppress the UI. Full shortcut table: references/rubric-author-flow.md.
expectations: Names the interactive grill as the default flow (not $EDITOR)
Mentions one-question-at-a-time with a concrete sample record
Lists the per-question shortcuts: k / d / free-text rewrite / + (add-mode jump)
Mentions batch shorthand like 'k 1-5 d 6,7' with explicit per-range prefix
Mentions add-mode at the end (empty line ends)
Mentions --editor as the escape hatch for non-interactive contexts
Notes that prompts write to stderr so the supaterm `> /dev/null` redirect on stdout doesn't hide the UI

evals.prompt: How do re-anchor and self-improve handle HITL? Do they block on stdin?
evals.expected_output: No — both use the export-decision-and-exit pattern (issue #232). The worker writes a *-decision-needed.json file with review context, exits with code 42, and the orchestrator (run.mts) propagates that to state.phase='hitl-pending' and exits 42 itself with a one-line operator pointer. The agent (Claude in chat) reads the needed-file, drives a grill conversation with concrete examples, and writes the spec'd decision file (re-anchor: {verdict, notes, rewrites}; self-improve: {corrected, reason}). Operator re-runs the same supaterm chain — confirmed bases no-op fast, the paused base detects + consumes (deletes) its decision file and proceeds. selfImprove is atomic: if any reject lacks a decision, it writes needed-files only for missing ones without consuming existing decisions. Legacy stdin / file-poll behavior is opt-in via --strict-stdin-hitl.
expectations: States the worker does NOT block on stdin or poll a file by default
Names the export-decision-and-exit pattern with exit code 42
Mentions decision-needed.json as the worker → agent payload
Mentions the agent (Claude in chat) drives the grill conversationally and writes the decision file
Mentions resume by re-running the same chain (worker consumes the decision file)
Notes that selfImprove is atomic — partial-decision case writes needed-files only for missing ones without consuming existing decisions
Mentions --strict-stdin-hitl as the opt-in flag for the legacy behavior

evals.prompt: The outcome log shows a `partial` result for an airtable-knowledge-extract run. What does this mean and what should the operator do?
evals.expected_output: partial maps to kind=hitl-pending — the HITL re-anchor gate exited with code 42 and awaits an operator decision. The operator should read the re-anchor-decision-needed.json file in <runDir>/hitl/, drive the grill conversation using the provided context, write the re-anchor-decision.json file with {verdict: approve|reject, notes}, and then re-run the same supaterm chain to resume.
expectations: States partial maps to kind=hitl-pending
Mentions exit code 42 as the hitl-pending signal
Mentions the operator must read the re-anchor-decision-needed.json file
Mentions writing the re-anchor-decision.json decision file to allow resume
Mentions re-running the same supaterm chain to resume

evals.prompt: The outcome log shows a `fail` result with escalation=re-anchor. What happened and what are the operator's next steps?
evals.expected_output: fail with escalation=re-anchor means kind=escalated-re-anchor — the operator reviewed the re-anchor shallow slice and rejected it (verdict=reject in the decision file). The base is not auto-promoted and must pass re-anchor on the next run-id. To retry: review what the shallow slice lacked via the re-anchor-decision-needed.json context, adjust the rubric via rubric-author.mts or fix the base schema, then re-run with a new run-id.
expectations: States fail with escalation=re-anchor maps to kind=escalated-re-anchor
States the operator rejected the re-anchor shallow slice
States the base is not auto-promoted — must pass re-anchor on a new run-id
Mentions fixing the underlying issue (rubric or base schema) before retrying

evals.prompt: The outcome log shows action=dispatch for an airtable-knowledge-extract run. What does this mean and what fields does it carry?
evals.expected_output: dispatch is a separate action type logged by scripts/dispatcher.mts (schema-driven dispatcher, #237) when it computes the component dispatch plan for a base. Output file: ~/.claude/airtable-extract-cache/<run-id>/dispatch-<base-id>.json. pass = plan computed; fail = unrecognized shape or no extractable tables. Optional fields: component_id (graph component id), shape (orphan|pair|chain|tree|hub-spoke|graph), records, est_tokens, strategy (edge-emit|single-pass|chunk-by-AP|record-by-record), interaction_handler.
expectations: Names `dispatch` as a distinct action type separate from `extract`
States `scripts/dispatcher.mts` writes the dispatch log entry
Mentions the output path `~/.claude/airtable-extract-cache/<run-id>/dispatch-<base-id>.json`
Lists the `shape` field values including hub-spoke
Lists the `strategy` field values including edge-emit

evals.prompt: What pre-steps must run before `bun run scripts/run.mts` for /airtable-knowledge-extract, and why?
evals.expected_output: Six per-base pre-steps in this order, all sharing AIRTABLE_RUN_ID: (1) classify-table-type — Stage 0 LLM table-type classifier (cached at ${SKILL_DIR}/cache/table-types/<base-id>.json, shared across runs); (2) classify-clusters — link-graph clusters via multipleRecordLinks; (3) cluster-classifier — cluster shapes (orphan|pair|chain|tree|hub-spoke|graph); (4) schema-analyser — natural keys + entity-table verdicts (depends on Stage 0); (5) legacy-link-detector — cross-cluster cleanup tasks; (6) seed-selection per cluster (inner jq loop over clusters-<base-id>.json — seed-selection.mts requires <base-id> <cluster-id> and exits non-zero if cluster-id is omitted). run.mts's internal dispatcher (added in #237/#268) then loads cluster-shapes-<base-id>.json + natural-keys-<base-id>.json + the Stage 0 cache and emits the dispatch plan; skipping any pre-step aborts run.mts with 'cache not found at ...' from the dispatcher.
expectations: Lists all six pre-steps in dependency order: classify-table-type, classify-clusters, cluster-classifier, schema-analyser, legacy-link-detector, seed-selection
States seed-selection.mts requires <base-id> <cluster-id> (per-cluster invocation, not per-base)
Mentions the inner jq loop over clusters-<base-id>.json to enumerate cluster IDs for seed-selection
Mentions that schema-analyser depends on the Stage 0 classify-table-type cache
States run.mts's internal dispatcher requires Stage 0 + cluster-shapes + natural-keys (so all four classifier pre-steps are mandatory)
Mentions the failure mode: skipping a pre-step aborts run.mts with 'cache not found' from the dispatcher
